commit.subject: LU-2533 osp: use OUT to batch syncrhonization
commit.message:
LU-2533 osp: use OUT to batch syncrhonization

wot

TODO:
 - is it OK to not cancel LDLM locks on OST object destroy?
   Jinshan said pages become clean and will be discarded at
   some point, but we also can discard them proactively when
   corresponding unlink is reported committed? discard the
   pages on the first ENOENT?
 - >1 in flight - need to serialize updates to same object
   probably not needed if batches are big enough? previously
   we had 8-in-flight by default, now we can easily have like
   few thousand
 - osd_destroy() to reset nlink (so no need in dt_ref_del()
   on OST objects at least
 - batch reconstruction
   currenlty the requests are idempotent given they are applied
   in the original order. probably we should skip reconstruction
   and execute again? a flag in OUT RPC preventing reconstruction?
 - improvements to OUT
   - dynamic reply size
   - can reply buffer be a list of bufs so we don't need to allocate
     many contigous pages?
 - error handling
 - additional stats
   - updates per batch
 - tests
  - something new?
  replay-single test_89: @@@@@@ FAIL: 6144 blocks leaked

Change-Id: Ic2a9033d6dffb5fccad464d19e9215606e59ec93
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
